questions.question: What does width: 50%; mean?
questions.explanation: Percentage width is calculated relative to the parent element's width.

questions.question: What is the difference between max-width and width?
options: max-width sets a limit; the element can be smaller
width sets a limit; max-width is the default
questions.explanation: max-width sets the maximum width the element can grow to, but it can be smaller. width sets an explicit size.

questions.question: Why might height: 100% not work on an element?
options: The parent element doesn't have an explicit height set
The element must be a block element
questions.explanation: Percentage height requires the parent to have an explicit height. If the parent's height is auto (default), 100% is undefined.

questions.question: What does vw stand for in CSS units?
questions.explanation: vw stands for viewport width, where 1vw equals 1% of the viewport's width.

questions.question: What is the default width behavior of a block-level element?
questions.explanation: Block-level elements default to width: auto, which fills the available horizontal space.

questions.question: What unit would you use to make an element half the viewport height?
questions.explanation: 50vh sets the height to 50% of the viewport height. vh = viewport height.

questions.question: What does overflow: hidden do when content exceeds an element's dimensions?
questions.explanation: overflow: hidden clips any content that extends beyond the element's boundaries without adding scrollbars.

questions.question: What does the ch unit measure?
options: Width of the '0' character in the current font
questions.explanation: The ch unit equals the width of the '0' (zero) glyph in the element's font, useful for setting line lengths.

questions.question: What does min-width: 320px do?
options: Sets the minimum width to 320px; the element can be wider
Prevents the element from being wider than 320px
questions.explanation: min-width sets a lower bound — the element can grow wider than 320px but cannot shrink below it.

questions.question: How do vmin and vmax units work?
options: vmin = smaller of vw/vh; vmax = larger of vw/vh
vmin = viewport minimum width; vmax = viewport maximum width
vmin = vertical minimum; vmax = vertical maximum
vmin = viewport minimum; vmax = viewport maximum
questions.explanation: vmin uses the smaller viewport dimension, vmax uses the larger. 50vmin = half the smaller viewport side.

questions.question: What is fit-content for width?
options: Shrinks to fit the content, but no larger than available space
Expands to fill available space regardless of content
questions.explanation: fit-content makes the element as wide as its content, but not wider than the available space in the container.

questions.question: What does aspect-ratio: 16/9 do?
options: Sets width to 16px and height to 9px
Maintains a 16:9 proportion between width and height
Creates a 16:9 aspect ratio for images only
Scales the element to 16 by 9 units
questions.explanation: aspect-ratio ensures the element maintains a 16:9 proportion based on one calculated dimension.

questions.question: When would max-height be useful?
options: To prevent an element from growing too tall
To make an element exactly a certain height
To set the minimum height of an element
questions.explanation: max-height limits how tall an element can grow, useful for expandable sections or preventing excessive height.

questions.question: What happens when both width and max-width are set?
options: max-width overrides width if width exceeds max-width
questions.explanation: max-width overrides width when the computed width would be larger than the max-width value.

questions.question: What is the default height of a block-level element with no content?
questions.explanation: A block element with no content has 0 height by default because height defaults to auto and there's nothing to size.

questions.question: How do you make an element fill the full viewport height?
questions.explanation: height: 100vh sets the element height to 100% of the viewport height, regardless of parent dimensions.

questions.question: What does width: auto do on an inline element?
options: Shrinks to fit the content width
questions.explanation: Inline elements with width: auto shrink-wrap to their content — they don't fill available space like block elements.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the aspect-ratio property?
options: To set the ratio of width to height for responsive scaling
To determine the screen aspect ratio
To set the image file format
To control the element's proportions on print
questions.explanation: aspect-ratio maintains a proportional relationship between width and height, useful for responsive images and videos.

questions.question: What does overflow: auto do?
options: Shows scrollbars only when content overflows
questions.explanation: overflow: auto adds scrollbars only when content exceeds the element's dimensions, keeping the layout clean otherwise.

questions.question: How do you ensure an image doesn't exceed its container width?
questions.explanation: max-width: 100% prevents the image from exceeding container width, and height: auto maintains aspect ratio.
